system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (straight duct along x-axis)

    (-50  25 -25)   // 1
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25  25)   // 3

    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6
    (  0 -25  25)   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    // Vertices 8-11

    ( 25   0 -25)   // 9
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 10
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 11

    // Block 2 - post-bend straight section
    // Vertices 12-15
    ( 25  -75  25)   // 12
    ( 25  -75 -25)   // 13
    ( 25 -125 -25)   // 14
    ( 25 -125  25)   // 15

    // Block 3 - extended outlet duct
    // Vertices 16-19
    (-500  -75  25)   // 16
    (-500  -75 -25)   // 17
    (-500 -125 -25)   // 18
    (-500 -125  25)   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    // Face 0-3 (left face, x=-50): 0,1,2,3 CCW
    // Face 4-7 (right face, x=0): 4,5,6,7 CCW
    // Edges: 0->4, 1->5, 2->6, 3->7
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    // Face 4-7 (left face, x=0): 4,5,6,7 CCW
    // Face 8-11 (right face, x=25): 8,9,10,11 CCW
    // Edges: 4->8, 5->9, 6->10, 7->11

    // Block 2: Post-bend straight section
    // Face 5-8 (bottom face, y=25): 5,6,7,8 CCW
    // Face 9-12 (top face, y=-75): 9,10,11,12 CCW
    // Edges: 5->9, 6->10, 7->11, 8->12
    hex (5 6 7 8 9 10 11 12) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: Extended outlet duct
    // Face 6-9 (top face, y=0): 6,7,8,9 CCW
    // Face 10-13 (bottom face, y=-125): 10,11,12,13 CCW

    hex (6 7 8 9 10 11 12 13) (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 17 18 19)
        );
    }

            (3 7 6 2)  // bottom face (z=-25)

            (5 9 10 6) // front face (y=-50)
            (4 5 6 7)  // top face (z=25)
            (8 11 10 9) // bottom face (z=-25)

            // Block 2 walls

            (6 7 11 10)  // front face (y=-75)
            (5 9 11 8)   // top face (z=25)
            (6 10 12 7)  // bottom face (z=-25)

            (7 11 12 8)  // front face (y=-125)
            (6 7 13 9)   // top face (z=25)
            (8 9 13 10)  // bottom face (z=-25)
        );
    }
);

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) is inside-out

The error message now shows:
```
Block hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) ... is inside-out
```

This confirms my previous diagnosis was correct — the vertex ordering needs to be fixed. However, looking more carefully at the current `blockMeshDict`, I notice the blocks are actually defined correctly as `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` (not the previously attempted `hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)`). So why is it still failing?

Let me examine the geometry more carefully. The vertices are:

- 0: (-50, 25, 25)
- 1: (-50, 25, -25)
- 2: (-50, -25, -25)
- 3: (-50, -25, 25)

Face 0-1-2-3 is at x = -50. Looking at this face from outside (negative x-direction), going 0→1→2→3: (-50,25,-25) → (-50,-25,-25) → (-50,-25,25). This goes downward then upward, which is clockwise when viewed from outside → wrong winding.

The fix is to reverse the order of vertices 0-3 to make them counter-clockwise when viewed from outside:

**Block 0 should be:**
```
hex (3 2 1 0 7 6 5 4) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
```

Similarly, all other blocks need their vertex ordering checked against the right-hand rule. The key principle: when viewing any face from the **outside** of the block, the vertices must go counter-clockwise.

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points"

This is purely a cascading error from `blockMesh` failing. Once `blockMesh` succeeds, this resolves automatically.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **Fix vertex ordering in all hex blocks** in `blockMeshDict` so that each block follows the right-hand rule (counter-clockwise when viewed from outside).
2. Update the **boundary face definitions** to match the corrected block topology.
3. All `0/` field files already use the correct patch names (`inlet`, `outlet`, `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`), so no changes needed there.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Change Block 0 hex definition from hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) to hex (3 2 1 0 7 6 5 4) to fix inside-out block; Change Block 1 hex definition from hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) to hex (7 6 5 4 11 10 9 8) to fix inside-out block; Change Block 2 hex definition from hex (5 6 7 8 9 10 11 12) to hex (8 7 6 5 12 11 10 9) to fix inside-out block; Change Block 3 hex definition from hex (6 7 8 9 10 11 12 13) to hex (9 8 7 6 13 12 11 10) to fix inside-out block; Reverse all boundary face definitions so they match the corrected block topology - update walls boundary faces to: Block 0 walls become (0 3 2 1) (4 7 6 5) (0 4 5 1) (3 7 6 2); Block 1 walls become (4 5 6 7) (8 9 10 11) (4 5 9 8) (7 6 10 11); Block 2 walls become (5 6 7 8) (9 10 11 12) (5 6 10 9) (7 8 11 12); Block 3 walls become (6 7 8 9) (10 11 12 13) (6 7 10 8) (9 11 12 13); Keep inlet and outlet patches unchanged')]
